entities.name: Bóng cờ vua
entities.description: Bóng cờ vua trắng dùng để đánh trong trò chơi bắn bóng.

entities.name: Bóng đối tượng
entities.description: 15 bóng đối tượng có các số từ 1 đến 15, được phân thành hai nhóm: bóng đồng (số 1-7) và bóng sọc (số 9-15). Bóng số 8 là màu đen.

entities.name: Bảng bắn bóng
entities.description: Một bàn bắn bóng vuông có kích thước 9 feet x 4.5 feet (2.74 m × 1.37 m), với sáu túi ở mỗi góc và giữa hai đường dài.

entities.name: Bảng đánh
entities.description: Một bề mặt chơi được làm từ đá slate phủ một lớp vải tơ chặt chẽ, thường là kết hợp của sợi lông và nylon. Màu sắc thường là xanh hoặc lá cây trong các trận đấu chuyên nghiệp.

entities.name: Trò chơi 8-ball
entities.description: Một loại trò chơi bắn bóng với 15 bóng đối tượng được phân thành hai nhóm: bóng đồng và bóng sọc, cùng với một bóng số 8.

entities.name: Trò chơi 9-ball
entities.description: Một loại trò chơi bắn bóng chỉ sử dụng 9 bóng đối tượng từ số 1 đến 9.

entities.name: Trò chơi 10-ball
entities.description: Một loại trò chơi bắn bóng chỉ sử dụng 10 bóng đối tượng từ số 1 đến 10.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object Ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls on the table that can be pocketed during a shot.

entities.name: Cue Stick
entities.description: The long stick used to strike the cue ball in billiards.

entities.name: Foul
entities.description: An infraction of the rules that results in a penalty.

entities.name: Game Format
entities.description: A specific variation of billiards, such as 8-ball, 9-ball, or 10-ball.

entities.name: Solid Ball
entities.description: One of the colored balls in a group of similar color used in 8-ball.

entities.name: Stripe Ball
entities.description: One of the colored balls in a group of alternating colors used in 8-ball.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where 15 balls are racked in a triangle.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where 9 balls are racked in a diamond shape.

entities.name: 10-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where 10 balls are racked in a triangle similar to 8-ball.

entities.name: ball
entities.description: The round objects used in billiards games.

entities.name: racking
entities.description: The process of arranging balls on the table before play begins.

entities.name: foot spot
entities.description: A designated spot at the foot of the billiards table where certain balls are placed.

entities.name: Lag
entities.description: A method to determine who breaks first in a billiards match.

entities.name: Head string
entities.description: The line on the table where balls are placed for the lag shot.

entities.name: Foot cushion
entities.description: One of the cushions at the bottom of the billiards table.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A specific game variant of billiards.

entities.name: solids
entities.description: One group of balls in 8-ball billiards, numbered 1-7.

entities.name: stripes
entities.description: Another group of balls in 8-ball billiards, numbered 9-15.

entities.name: break shot
entities.description: The initial shot at the start of a game in billiards.

entities.name: pocket
entities.description: Action of hitting a ball into a corner pocket to remove it from play.

entities.name: Break shot
entities.description: The opening shot of the game.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Head string
entities.description: A line on the table used for positioning the cue ball and balls during a break shot.

entities.name: Rack
entities.description: The device used to arrange balls before a game in billiards.

entities.name: Object balls
entities.description: Balls other than the cue ball and 8-ball used in billiards.

entities.name: Cushions
entities.description: The sides of the table that balls can hit during play.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A ball with a solid color used in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: Ball in hand
entities.description: The incoming player's right to choose any ball on the table as their next shot.

entities.name: Break
entities.description: The initial shot at the start of a game in billiards.

entities.name: Open Table
entities.description: A state where neither player has been assigned solids or stripes after the break.

entities.name: Solids
entities.description: The numbered balls in billiards, typically 1 through 7.

entities.name: Stripes
entities.description: The colored balls in billiards, typically 9 through 15.

entities.name: Group Assignment
entities.description: The process of assigning groups (solids or stripes) to players based on their first legal shot after the break.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A billiard ball used in 8-ball pool.

entities.name: shooter's group
entities.description: The set of balls a player is trying to pocket first in 8-ball pool.

entities.name: pocket
entities.description: A hole on the billiard table where balls can be pocketed.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: An illegal action in billiards that results in a penalty.

entities.name: Combination shots
entities.description: A legal shot in 8-ball billiards where a player uses any ball on the table to create a combination, including opponent's balls or the 8-ball.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used by players in billiards to strike other balls.

entities.name: Solid balls
entities.description: A group of numbered balls (1-7) in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: Striped balls
entities.description: A group of numbered balls (9-15) in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: The black ball used as the target ball in 8-ball billiards.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use numbered balls to achieve specific objectives.

entities.name: ball in hand
entities.description: A penalty in 8-ball where the incoming player is given a free shot with the cue ball on the table.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The primary striking ball used in billiards, including 8-ball.

entities.name: legal ball
entities.description: A ball that can be legally hit during a shot in 8-ball.

entities.name: cushion
entities.description: The boundary of the billiard table where balls can pocketed or bounce off.

entities.name: pocketed
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where a ball is accidentally sent into one of the pockets.

entities.name: double hit
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where the cue ball is struck more than once during a shot.

entities.name: push shot
entities.description: A foul in 8-ball where the cue ball is pushed instead of being struck with the cue stick.

entities.name: turn
entities.description: A sequence of shots made by a single player before passing to the opponent.

entities.name: Foul
entities.description: A foul in billiards where a player fails to make a legal shot.

entities.name: Game
entities.description: The overall match or session of billiards.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A specific game variant in billiards.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: lowest numbered ball
entities.description: The ball with the lowest number on the table that must be contacted first when shooting.

entities.name: Break Shot
entities.description: The initial shot made by a player at the start of a game.

entities.name: Push Out
entities.description: A strategy where a player hits the cue ball in any direction without contacting the lowest numbered ball first or driving any ball to a cushion immediately after the break shot.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The white ball used for striking other balls in billiards.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: head string
entities.description: A line on the table that marks where the cue ball should be placed for a break shot.

entities.name: 1-ball
entities.description: The first ball in the rack, typically white and numbered 1.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Balls numbered from 2 to 9 used in billiards games like 9-ball.

entities.name: cushions
entities.description: The sides and ends of the table that balls can hit during play.

entities.name: break shot
entities.description: The opening shot in a game, where the cue ball is struck to start play.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A specific object ball used in 9-ball billiards.

extractions.chunk_title: Winning by Pocketing the 9-Ball on the Break

entities.name: Breaker
entities.description: Player who attempts to pocket balls on the break shot.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: The highest-value ball in 9-ball billiards.

entities.name: Legal Break
entities.description: Break shot that meets the game's requirements, allowing the player to pocket balls on the break.

entities.name: Illegal Break
entities.description: Break shot that does not meet the game's requirements, resulting in a penalty.

entities.name: Ball in Hand
entities.description: Position where a player has the option to choose any ball on the table for their next shot.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use a cue ball to pocket balls in ascending order.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The primary ball used for striking other balls in billiards.

entities.name: incoming player
entities.description: The player who receives the cue after a foul or successful shot.

entities.name: foot spot
entities.description: A designated area on the table where balls are placed when they are spotted.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use a cue ball and numbered balls to score points.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: An infraction in the rules of billiards, typically resulting in penalty points or loss of turn.

entities.name: loss of game
entities.description: The outcome when a player accumulates a specified number of fouls and is declared the loser.

entities.name: 10-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards using 10 balls instead of 9.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards using 9 balls.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: rack
entities.description: The triangular frame used to arrange the balls at the start of a game.

entities.name: shot
entities.description: A single attempt by a player to hit one or more balls into pockets.

entities.name: pocket
entities.description: One of the six holes on the billiard table where balls can be legally pocketed.

entities.name: call shot
entities.description: A requirement in 10-ball where the shooter must specify the ball and pocket for each shot.

entities.name: obvious shot
entities.description: A shot that is easy to predict or execute without needing to be called.

entities.name: slop shot
entities.description: An unintentional pocketing of a ball during a shot.

entities.name: 10-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players must call their intended shot before each turn.

entities.name: shot
entities.description: The action of hitting the cue ball to make a ball fall into a pocket.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used as the starting point for each shot in billiards.

entities.name: ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls numbered 1 through 15 used in billiards.

entities.name: pocket
entities.description: One of six holes on the table where balls are aimed to be pocketed.

entities.name: cushion
entities.description: The sides and railings of the billiards table that balls can hit during a shot.

entities.name: Push-out
entities.description: A rule allowing a player to hit the cue ball in any direction after declaring a push out during the break.

entities.name: 10-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players aim to pocket balls in numerical order, starting with the 1 object ball and ending with the 10 ball.

entities.name: 9-ball
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players aim to pocket balls in numerical order, starting with the 1 object ball and ending with the 9 ball.

entities.name: Break shot
entities.description: The opening shot at the start of a game in billiards, where players aim to pocket balls on the break.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used for striking other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Head string
entities.description: A line on the table that marks the boundary of the shooting area.

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards played on a pocketless table.

entities.name: carom billiards
entities.description: An alternative name for 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: bida 3 bang
entities.description: The Vietnamese term for 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: pocketless table
entities.description: A table without pockets used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: standard competition table
entities.description: The official size of a 3-cushion billiards table for competitions.

entities.name: 9-foot tables
entities.description: An alternative table size used in recreational play.

entities.name: heated playing surface
entities.description: A feature of the table to reduce moisture and ensure consistent ball behavior.

entities.name: Simonis or similar high-quality cloth
entities.description: The covering material for the table's playing surface.

entities.name: rail height and cushion profile
entities.description: Standardized specifications to ensure consistent rebound angles.

entities.name: diamond markers
entities.description: Reference points for aiming and the diamond system in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: billiard spots
entities.description: Another term for diamond markers used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: pins
entities.description: Yet another term for diamond markers used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A mathematical aiming method using the diamond markers.

entities.name: white cue ball
entities.description: The primary cue ball used in 3-cushion billiards, often shared between players.

entities.name: yellow object ball
entities.description: One of the two colored balls used as targets in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: red object ball
entities.description: The other colored ball used as a target in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: international competition
entities.description: A type of competition where each player has their own white and yellow cue balls.

extractions.chunk_title: Object of the Game and Scoring

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards where the objective is to make the cue ball contact at least three cushions before hitting the second object ball.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The main striking ball in billiards.

entities.name: cushions (rails)
entities.description: The rails around the table that the cue ball must contact to score points.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: A ball hit by the cue ball during gameplay.

entities.name: point
entities.description: The scoring unit in billiards, awarded for successfully hitting the object ball after contacting at least three cushions.

entities.name: natural shot
entities.description: A scoring pattern where two short rails and one long rail are used to score points.

entities.name: plus two diamond system shots
entities.description: Scoring patterns involving specific shots that result in additional points.

entities.name: plus three pattern
entities.description: A scoring pattern where the cue ball contacts three cushions to score points.

entities.name: world record average
entities.description: The highest average score achieved in official competitions.

extractions.chunk_title: Common Patterns and Diamond System Applications

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards where players use diamond markers to calculate angles for multi-cushion shots.

entities.name: diamond markers
entities.description: Markers on the rails used in 3-cushion billiards to calculate angles for shots.

entities.name: plus 2 system
entities.description: A mathematical pattern used in 3-cushion billiards where starting and ending diamond positions add up to 2.

entities.name: plus 3 system
entities.description: Another mathematical pattern used in 3-cushion billiards for shorter rail travel, cross-table shots, and end-rail patterns.

entities.name: feel
entities.description: A skill developed by experienced players to adjust their shots based on various factors like speed, spin, cloth conditions, and ball positions.

entities.name: Foul
entities.description: A violation of the rules during a game of 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: Any colored ball that can be hit during a game of billiards.

entities.name: Cushion
entities.description: The rail around the table in billiards.

entities.name: Foot spot
entities.description: A designated area on the table where balls are spotted after being driven off.

entities.name: Center spot
entities.description: The center of the table, used as an alternative spot if the foot spot is occupied.

entities.name: Scratch
entities.description: A foul where the cue ball is pocketed.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Incoming Player
entities.description: The player who will take the next shot after a foul.

entities.name: Head String
entities.description: A line on the table used to mark where balls should be placed in some traditional rulesets.

entities.name: 3-Cushion Billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards played with three cushions.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: A foul occurs when an object ball is driven off the table.

entities.name: foot spot
entities.description: A specific point on the table where balls are placed.

entities.name: incoming player
entities.description: The player who will take the next shot after a foul.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: starting position
entities.description: The initial position of the cue ball on the table.

entities.name: Double hit
entities.description: A foul in billiards where the cue tip strikes the cue ball more than once on a single shot.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used as the target in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: Any colored ball used as a target in billiards except the cue ball.

entities.name: Elevated cue
entities.description: A playing technique where the cue is raised above the table to avoid double hits.

entities.name: Smooth, continuous stroke
entities.description: A playing technique where the cue is struck smoothly and continuously to avoid double hits.

entities.name: Push Shot
entities.description: A playing technique where the cue tip remains in contact with the cue ball for an extended period during the stroke.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The main ball used in billiards, struck by the cue stick to score points.

entities.name: Object Ball
entities.description: A ball other than the cue ball that can be pocketed for points in billiards.

entities.name: Cue Stick
entities.description: The stick used to strike the cue ball and object balls in billiards.

entities.name: Rule
entities.description: A specific rule or regulation of billiards.

entities.name: Stroke
entities.description: The action of hitting the cue ball with a cue stick in billiards.

entities.name: Floor
entities.description: The ground on which the billiard table is placed.

entities.name: Cue Tip
entities.description: The tip of the cue stick used to hit the cue ball in billiards.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The white ball at the center of a billiard table, which players aim to pocket during gameplay.

entities.name: Referee
entities.description: A person responsible for enforcing rules and maintaining order in competitive sports.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: A foul occurs when a player touches, moves, or disturbs any ball on the table other than through a legal shot.

entities.name: cue stick
entities.description: The tool used to strike the balls in billiards.

entities.name: hand
entities.description: A part of a player's body used for handling equipment or touching the table.

entities.description: A part of a player's body used for handling equipment or touching the table.

entities.name: clothing
entities.description: Wearables that may come into contact with the ball during play.

entities.name: ball
entities.description: A round object used in billiards to be struck by the cue stick.

entities.name: gust of wind
entities.description: An external factor that may move a ball unintentionally.

entities.name: vibrating table
entities.description: A condition where the table shakes or moves, potentially moving balls unintentionally.

entities.name: practice stroke

entities.name: referee
entities.description: An official who makes decisions during the game, including ruling on fouls and ball positions.

entities.name: foul
entities.description: A foul occurs when a player shoots when it is not their turn.

entities.name: opponent
entities.description: The other player in the game.

entities.name: balls
entities.description: The objects used in billiards to score points.

entities.name: Billiards
entities.description: A sport played with a cue stick and balls on a table.

entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls used in billiards except the cue ball.

entities.name: Head string
entities.description: A line on the table that defines a restricted area for placing the cue ball after an opponent's foul.

entities.name: WPA rules
entities.description: The World Pool-Billiard Association's set of rules governing various billiards games.

entities.name: 8-ball
entities.description: A variant of pool where players must pocket balls in a specific order to win.

entities.description: A variant of pool where players must pocket balls in numerical order to win.
